initial_program:
% Input dimensions
n_rows(N) :- #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M)  :- #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Output size (the grid is square)
outputSize(S) :- S = N * N.

% Block size
B = N + 1.

% Output cell coordinates
row(R) :- R >= 0, R < N * N.
col(C) :- C >= 0, C < N * N.

% Block indices and offsets
i = R // B.
j = C // B.
p = R - i * B.
q = C - j * B.

% ----- maximum colour in the input -----
higher_colour(C) :- input(I,J,Y), Y > C.
max_colour(C)      :- input(I,J,C), not higher_colour(C).
#count{ C : max_colour(C) } = 1.

% ----- highest diagonal index where the max colour appears -----
higher_max_diag(K) :- input(J,J,MaxCol), J > K.
max_diag_index(K)  :- input(K,K,MaxCol), not higher_max_diag(K).
#count{ K : max_diag_index(K) } = 1.

% ----- cells that receive the max colour -----
% Full diagonal block (i=j) when i  K (i.e. i  highest index with max colour)
max_cell(R,C) :- i = j, input(i,i,V), V != 0, i >= K, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Interior of lower diagonal blocks (i=j, i < K)
max_cell(R,C) :- i = j, p >= 1, q >= 1, i < K, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Superdiagonal block (i,i+1) for i = K2
max_cell(R,C) :- j = i + 1, i = K - 2, p >= 1, q <= 1, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Cells that should be zero
output(R,C,0) :- not max_cell(R,C).

% Output cell definition
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

% Input dimensions
n_rows(N) :- #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M)  :- #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Output size (the grid is square)
outputSize(S) :- S = N * N.

% Block size
B = N + 1.

% Output cell coordinates
row(R) :- R >= 0, R < N * N.
col(C) :- C >= 0, C < N * N.

% Block indices and offsets
i = R div B.
j = C div B.
p = R - i * B.
q = C - j * B.

% ----- maximum colour in the input -----
higher_colour(C) :- input(I,J,Y), Y > C.
max_colour(C)      :- input(I,J,C), not higher_colour(C).
#count{ C : max_colour(C) } = 1.

% ----- highest diagonal index where the max colour appears -----
higher_max_diag(K) :- input(J,J,MaxCol), J > K.
max_diag_index(K)  :- input(K,K,MaxCol), not higher_max_diag(K).
#count{ K : max_diag_index(K) } = 1.

% ----- cells that receive the max colour -----
% Full diagonal block (i=j) when i  K (i.e. i  highest index with max colour)
max_cell(R,C) :- i = j, input(i,i,V), V != 0, i >= K, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Interior of lower diagonal blocks (i=j, i < K)
max_cell(R,C) :- i = j, p >= 1, q >= 1, i < K, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Superdiagonal block (i,i+1) for i = K2
max_cell(R,C) :- j = i + 1, i = K - 2, p >= 1, q <= 1, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Cells that should be zero
output(R,C,0) :- not max_cell(R,C).

% Output cell definition
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

final_program:
% Input dimensions
n_rows(N) :- #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M)  :- #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

% Output size (the grid is square)
outputSize(S) :- S = N * N.

% Block size
B = N + 1.

% Output cell coordinates
row(R) :- R >= 0, R < N * N.
col(C) :- C >= 0, C < N * N.

% Block indices and offsets
i = R div B.
j = C div B.
p = R - i * B.
q = C - j * B.

% ----- maximum colour in the input -----
higher_colour(C) :- input(I,J,Y), Y > C.
max_colour(C)      :- input(I,J,C), not higher_colour(C).
#count{ C : max_colour(C) } = 1.

% ----- highest diagonal index where the max colour appears -----
higher_max_diag(K) :- input(J,J,MaxCol), J > K.
max_diag_index(K)  :- input(K,K,MaxCol), not higher_max_diag(K).
#count{ K : max_diag_index(K) } = 1.

% ----- cells that receive the max colour -----
% Full diagonal block (i=j) when i  K (i.e. i  highest index with max colour)
max_cell(R,C) :- i = j, input(i,i,V), V != 0, i >= K, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Interior of lower diagonal blocks (i=j, i < K)
max_cell(R,C) :- i = j, p >= 1, q >= 1, i < K, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Superdiagonal block (i,i+1) for i = K2
max_cell(R,C) :- j = i + 1, i = K - 2, p >= 1, q <= 1, Color = MaxCol, max_colour(MaxCol).

% Cells that should be zero
output(R,C,0) :- not max_cell(R,C).

% Output cell definition
output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.
